title: Database migration: tournament_providers and tournaments tables
description: Create the database tables for tracking CourtHive tournament providers and their tournaments. Providers map to CourtHive provider abbreviations (e.g. St Ann's, Parks League Cup). Tournaments reference a provider and store the CourtHive tournament ID, with a visibility flag (hidden by default) to control what appears on the public index.
acceptance_criteria: New migration creates tournament_providers table with id, name, provider_abbr (unique), created_at, updated_at

New migration creates tournaments table with id, name, description, courthive_tournament_id (unique), provider_id (FK), start_date, end_date, is_visible (default FALSE), display_order (default 0), created_at, updated_at
Down migration drops both tables in correct order (tournaments first, then providers)
Migration runs cleanly on existing database
Model structs added to internal/models/models.go: TournamentProvider and Tournament

approach: 1. Create up migration with both tables, foreign key from tournaments.provider_id to tournament_providers.id
2. Add index on tournaments.is_visible for efficient public page queries
3. Add index on tournaments.courthive_tournament_id for sync lookups
4. Create down migration dropping tables in dependency order
5. Add TournamentProvider struct: ID, Name, ProviderAbbr, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt
6. Add Tournament struct: ID, Name, Description, CourthiveTournamentID, ProviderID, StartDate, EndDate, IsVisible, DisplayOrder, CreatedAt, UpdatedAt

testing_requirements: Migration applies cleanly to existing database
Foreign key constraint enforced (cannot insert tournament with invalid provider_id)
Unique constraint on provider_abbr and courthive_tournament_id enforced

context.user_story: As a system foundation, we need tables to store CourtHive provider and tournament data so the admin can manage which tournaments are publicly visible
